subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Mighty Cable Company Cost 6√x $/meter, Revenue $120/meter — Profit, Integral Meaning, Expression & Max Profit
stimulus: Mighty Cable Company manufactures cable that sells for $120 per meter. For a cable of fixed length, the cost of producing a portion of the cable varies with its distance from the beginning of the cable. Mighty reports that the cost to produce a portion of a cable that is $x$ meters from the beginning of the cable is $6\sqrt{x}$ dollars per meter. (Note: Profit is defined to be the difference between the amount of money received by the company for selling the cable and the company's cost of producing the cable.)

parts.prompt: Find Mighty's profit on the sale of a 25-meter cable.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up correct integral for profit: $120 \cdot 25 - \int_0^{25} 6\sqrt{x}\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Profit $= 120 \cdot 25 - \int_0^{25} 6\sqrt{x}\, dx = 3000 - 500 = 2500$ dollars.

parts.prompt: Using correct units, explain the meaning of $\int_{25}^{30} 6\sqrt{x}\, dx$ in the context of this problem.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct explanation with units: $\int_{25}^{30} 6\sqrt{x}\, dx$ is the difference in cost, in dollars, of producing a cable of length 30 meters and a cable of length 25 meters (i.e., the additional cost to extend the cable from 25 to 30 meters)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_{25}^{30} 6\sqrt{x}\, dx$ is the difference in cost, in dollars, of producing a cable of length 30 meters versus a cable of length 25 meters.

parts.prompt: Write an expression, involving an integral, that represents Mighty's profit on the sale of a cable that is $k$ meters long.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct profit expression: $P(k) = 120k - \int_0^k 6\sqrt{x}\, dx$ dollars
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Profit $= 120k - \int_0^k 6\sqrt{x}\, dx$ dollars.

parts.prompt: Find the maximum profit that Mighty could earn on the sale of one cable. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Justification: $P'(k) > 0$ for $0 < k < 400$ and $P'(k) < 0$ for $k > 400$, so $P$ has a maximum at $k = 400$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $P'(k) = 120 - 6\sqrt{k} = 0 \Rightarrow k = 400$. Maximum profit $P(400) = 16000$ dollars. $P'$ changes from positive to negative at $k = 400$.
